title: retarget-run output data
description:
The retarget outcome — an S2-shaped brief + the supersession audit.

``stage_reached`` is the re-canary outcome the human decides on:
``retargeted_canary_verified`` (green on the new cluster — greenlight S3),
``retargeted_canary_failed`` (failed AGAIN — the human picks the next
recovery), or ``resolve_blocked`` (the fresh resolve surfaced its own
decision, e.g. a live sibling of the NEW run_id from a prior retarget).
``needs_decision`` is always True: the human re-``y``s the brief through the
EXISTING ``append-decision`` path — this verb produces the brief, it does NOT
bypass the gates. ``superseded_run_id`` / ``applied_patch`` are the audit of
what the retarget closed and changed.

stage_reached.description: The re-canary outcome the amended brief stops at.
stage_reached.title: Stage Reached

needs_decision.description: Always True — the human re-y's the retarget brief through append-decision.
needs_decision.title: Needs Decision

reason.description: Human-readable one-line summary of the retarget outcome.
reason.title: Reason

superseded_run_id.description: The failed attempt closed by wave-2 supersession (old→new link stamped).
superseded_run_id.title: Superseded Run Id

run_id.description: The retargeted run's run_id (<new_run_name>-<cmd_sha[:8]>); None if unresolved.
run_id.title: Run Id

verified.description: True iff the re-canary verified green on the NEW cluster (#160 gate).
verified.title: Verified

failure_kind.description: The canary failure kind when the re-canary failed on the new cluster; else None.
failure_kind.title: Failure Kind

brief.description: The S2-shaped brief: the retargeted resolved values + the re-canary result (run_id, cluster, verified, est core-hours) + the supersession summary. The LLM relays it and takes the human's re-y.
brief.title: Brief

applied_patch.description: The delta actually applied {field: value} — the audit of what the retarget changed.
applied_patch.title: Applied Patch
